title: Kitesurfing in Tarifa — Complete Spot Guide 2026
meta_description: Kitesurfing in Tarifa: best spots, wind seasons, gear advice & local tips. Plan your Tarifa kite trip with Surf Store's expert guide.
meta_keywords: kitesurfing tarifa,tarifa kite spots,tarifa wind,kite trip tarifa
body_html:
Why Tarifa Is Europe's Kite Capital

Tarifa sits at the southernmost tip of Spain where the Atlantic meets the Mediterranean. This geographic funnel creates two dominant wind patterns — the Levante (east) and Poniente (west) — that deliver rideable wind on 300+ days per year. No other spot in Europe comes close to that consistency.

The town itself is compact, walkable, and built around kitesurfing culture. Kite schools, board shops, and kite-friendly accommodation line the beachfront. From June to September, the beaches fill with kites from dawn to dusk.

Best Kite Spots in Tarifa

Los Lances: The main beach — 6km of flat, sandy bottom with consistent side-shore wind. Best for beginners and freeriders. Lifeguard-patrolled zones keep swimmers separate. Shallow water extends 200m+ offshore.

Valdevaqueros: 10 minutes north of town. Stronger, gustier wind (Levante channels between hills). More advanced conditions — popular with wave riders when westerly swell combines with Poniente wind. Sandy bottom with some rocks at the edges.

Balneario: The spot closest to town. Smaller beach, thermal wind in summer afternoons. Good for quick sessions but crowded in peak season. Side-shore Poniente is the most common direction.

Wind Seasons

MonthAvg Wind (kts)DirectionWater TempRating
Jan–Mar15–25Levante/Poniente mix15–16°CGood (cold)
Apr–May18–28Levante dominant17–19°CExcellent
Jun–Aug20–35Strong Levante20–23°CPeak season
Sep–Oct15–25Poniente returns20–22°CExcellent
Nov–Dec12–22Variable16–18°CGood (quieter)

What Gear to Bring

Tarifa's wind is strong and gusty, especially in Levante conditions. Bring your smaller kites — a 7m and 9m cover most summer sessions for an 80kg rider. A 12m is useful for lighter Poniente days. A 3/2mm wetsuit handles April–October; bring a 4/3mm for winter.

Board choice: a standard freeride twin-tip (136–140cm) handles all conditions. Bring a surfboard if you ride Valdevaqueros in westerly swell.

FAQ

Q: When is the best time to kitesurf in Tarifa?
A: June–September for strongest, most consistent wind. April–May and September–October for warm conditions with fewer crowds.

Q: Is Tarifa good for beginners?
A: Yes — Los Lances is one of Europe's best learning spots. Multiple IKO-certified schools operate year-round.

Q: Do I need a car in Tarifa?
A: Helpful for reaching Valdevaqueros, but Los Lances and Balneario are walkable from town. Many riders bike with kite bags.
excerpt: Complete guide to kitesurfing in Tarifa: spots, wind chart, gear tips, and travel advice.

title: Kitesurfing in Fuerteventura — Spot & Wind Guide
meta_description: Kitesurfing Fuerteventura: Sotavento, Flag Beach & more. Wind chart, gear tips & travel advice. Plan your trip with Surf Store.
meta_keywords: kitesurfing fuerteventura,fuerteventura kite spots,sotavento kite,flag beach kite
body_html:
Fuerteventura — Year-Round Kite Paradise

Fuerteventura is the most wind-reliable island in the Canary Islands, sitting in the path of the northeast trade winds that blow consistently from April to October. Unlike mainland Spain, the island's flat terrain and offshore position create clean, laminar wind that rarely gusts — making it one of the most comfortable places to kitesurf in the world.

The island's south coast hosts the famous Sotavento lagoon — a shallow, flat-water playground that appears at low tide and has hosted PWA and GKA World Tour events for decades.

Best Kite Spots

Sotavento (Playa Barca): The jewel. At low tide, a 1km-wide lagoon forms with ankle-to-knee-deep water — perfect flat water for freestyle, foiling, and learning. At high tide, the lagoon disappears and conditions become choppy open ocean. Check tide tables before every session.

Flag Beach (Corralejo): The north coast spot. Consistent side-shore trade wind, sandy bottom, and a relaxed vibe. Less crowded than Sotavento. Slightly gustier in strong trade wind due to the town's buildings creating turbulence at the edge.

El Cotillo: West coast lagoons with turquoise water. Works in south wind (less common but excellent when it blows). Protected from the trade wind — good when Sotavento is overpowered.

Wind Chart

MonthAvg WindDirectionWater TempRating
Jan–Mar15–22NE trades18–19°CGood
Apr–Jun20–30NE trades (strong)19–21°CExcellent
Jul–Sep22–35NE trades (peak)22–24°CPeak
Oct–Dec15–25NE trades (fading)21–22°CGood

Gear Recommendations

Trade winds blow strong and steady. Core quiver for 80kg: 7m + 9m kites cover 90% of sessions May–September. Add a 12m for winter light-wind days. A 3/2mm wetsuit or even boardshorts work June–October; 4/3mm for winter mornings.

Sotavento's flat water is ideal for foil kiting — bring your foil setup if you have one.

FAQ

Q: Best month for Fuerteventura kitesurfing?
A: July–August for maximum wind. May–June for warm weather with strong wind and fewer tourists.

Q: Is Sotavento suitable for beginners?
A: The low-tide lagoon is one of the world's best beginner spots — flat, shallow, sandy bottom. Multiple schools operate daily.

Q: Do I need to rent a car?
A: Yes — spots are spread across the island. A small rental car costs €20–30/day and is essential for chasing the best conditions.

title: Windsurfing at Lake Garda — Spot & Wind Guide
meta_description: Windsurfing Lake Garda: Torbole, Malcesine, wind patterns, gear tips. Europe's most famous inland windsurf spot. Surf Store guide.
meta_keywords: windsurfing lake garda,lake garda windsurf,torbole windsurfing,malcesine windsurf
body_html:
Lake Garda — Europe's Windsurf Mecca

Lake Garda in northern Italy has been the spiritual home of European windsurfing for 40 years. The lake's unique geography — a narrow northern basin flanked by 2,000m mountains — creates two reliable thermal winds: the Peler in the morning and the Ora in the afternoon. This double-wind system makes Garda one of the most consistent windsurf spots in the world, with rideable conditions on 250+ days per year.

The northern towns of Torbole and Malcesine are the main windsurf hubs, with launch areas, gear storage, and a deep windsurfing culture that permeates every restaurant and bar along the waterfront.

The Winds of Lake Garda

Peler (morning): A north wind that builds from midnight and peaks at 8–11 AM. Typically 12–20 knots, strongest in summer. The Peler blows down the lake from north to south — creating flat water at Torbole and small chop further south.

Ora (afternoon): A south-southwest thermal that builds from noon and peaks at 2–5 PM. Typically 15–25 knots in summer. The Ora creates more chop than the Peler and is generally stronger — preferred by intermediate and advanced riders.

Best Spots

Torbole — Conca d'Oro: The primary launch. Flat water in Peler, choppier in Ora. Sandy/gravel beach with easy water access. Crowded in peak season — arrive early for space.

Malcesine: 15 minutes south. Similar wind but less crowded. Excellent Ora conditions with cross-shore wind. Several gear rental and storage facilities.

Campione del Garda: West shore. Works in Ora with clean cross-shore. Popular with racers for flat-water speed runs.

Gear for Lake Garda

Lake Garda wind is moderate and thermal — not as strong as ocean spots. For an 80kg rider:

  • Sails: 6.0–7.5m covers most sessions. Bring an 8.0m+ for light Peler days
  • Board: 120–140L freeride or freewave. Smaller volumes for Ora sessions
  • Wetsuit: 3/2mm spring–autumn, 4/3mm for Peler sessions (water is cold: 12–16°C in spring)

FAQ

Q: Best time to windsurf Lake Garda?
A: June–September for strongest and most reliable thermal winds. April–May for uncrowded Peler sessions.

Q: Is Lake Garda good for beginners?
A: The Peler is manageable for intermediates. True beginners should take lessons — several schools operate in Torbole.

Q: Is Lake Garda water cold?
A: Yes — 12–16°C in spring, 18–22°C in peak summer. A wetsuit is required year-round for extended sessions.
excerpt: Complete guide to windsurfing at Lake Garda: Torbole, Malcesine, wind patterns, and gear recommendations.

title: Kitesurfing in Dakhla Morocco — Lagoon & Wind Guide
meta_description: Kitesurfing Dakhla Morocco: flat-water lagoon, year-round wind, gear tips. The ultimate kite destination. Surf Store guide.
meta_keywords: kitesurfing dakhla,dakhla kite lagoon,morocco kitesurfing,dakhla wind
body_html:
Dakhla — The World's Best Flat Water

Dakhla is a narrow peninsula on Morocco's Atlantic Saharan coast that creates a 40km-long lagoon with butter-flat water, consistent 20–30 knot trade winds, and warm air temperatures year-round. For kitesurfers — especially freestyle riders, foilers, and speed enthusiasts — Dakhla is as close to perfect as any spot on earth.

The lagoon's shallow depth (waist-deep in many areas), sandy bottom, and absence of waves create conditions that make even moderate skills look impressive. Add 300+ wind days per year and you understand why Dakhla has become the winter escape for every serious European kitesurfer.

Main Spots

Speed Spot / White Dune: The most famous area. A long, flat sandbar creates a speed strip with side-shore wind and flat water. Popular for speed runs, freestyle, and foiling. Kite camps line the shore with equipment storage and launch assistance.

Lassarga: Further south in the lagoon. Less crowded, similar wind. Wider riding area for downwinders and long reaches. Some camps offer boat support for retrieving riders.

PK25: 25km south on the open Atlantic side. Wave spot with cross-shore wind and consistent swell. For experienced riders only — reef bottom and strong currents.

Wind & Weather

MonthWind (kts)Air TempWater TempRating
Jan–Mar18–2820–24°C17–18°CExcellent
Apr–Jun22–3222–28°C18–20°CPeak
Jul–Sep20–3028–35°C20–22°CPeak (hot)
Oct–Dec18–2822–28°C19–21°CExcellent

Gear Tips

Dakhla wind is strong and consistent. Core quiver: 7m + 9m for 80kg rider. A 5m–6m for the strongest days. Boardshorts or a 2mm shorty are sufficient March–November. 3/2mm wetsuit for winter mornings when the Saharan air is cool before sunrise.

Foil kiting is exceptional in the lagoon — bring your foil setup. The flat water and consistent wind make Dakhla the ideal foil training ground.

FAQ

Q: Do I need a visa for Morocco?
A: EU citizens get 90 days visa-free. Dakhla has direct flights from several European cities via Casablanca.

Q: Is Dakhla safe?
A: Yes — the kite camp areas are secure and welcoming. Dakhla is a peaceful town with a strong tourism infrastructure built around kitesurfing.

Q: Can I rent gear in Dakhla?
A: Yes — most camps offer full kite rental. But bringing your own gear is recommended for the best experience.

title: Best Wing Foil Spots in Europe — Top 10 Guide
meta_title: Best Wing Foil Spots Europe 2026 — Top 10
meta_description: Top 10 wing foil spots in Europe: from Lake Garda to Tarifa. Wind, conditions & gear tips for each location. Surf Store guide.
meta_keywords: wing foil spots europe,best wingfoil spots,where to wing foil europe
body_html:
Top 10 Wing Foil Spots in Europe

Wing foiling works in lighter wind than kitesurfing and needs less setup than windsurfing — making it ideal for spots that were previously considered marginal. Here are Europe's 10 best wing foil destinations, ranked by wind reliability, water conditions, and overall experience.

1. Lake Garda, Italy

The Ora thermal delivers 15–25 knots on most summer afternoons. Flat water in the morning (Peler), moderate chop in the afternoon. The 18–22°C summer water temperature is comfortable with a 3/2mm suit. Multiple foil-friendly launch spots in Torbole and Malcesine.

2. Tarifa, Spain

Europe's wind capital works for wing foiling year-round. Los Lances offers flat water in Poniente wind. The strong Levante gives experienced wing foilers high-speed sessions. Water is warm (20–23°C summer) with sandy bottom.

3. Leucate, France

The Tramontane wind blows 15–30 knots from the northwest on 200+ days per year. The shallow lagoon at Leucate-La Franqui is ideal for learning to wing foil — flat, warm, and waist-deep.

4. Silvaplana, Switzerland

Alpine lake with reliable Maloja thermal wind in summer. Crystal-clear water, stunning mountain scenery, and a growing wing foil community. Cold water (15–18°C) requires a 4/3mm suit.

5. Alacati, Turkey

Consistent Meltemi wind from June to September. The shallow bay has flat water and warm temperatures (25–28°C). One of the most affordable destinations with excellent Turkish hospitality.

6. Dahab, Egypt

Year-round thermal wind in the Gulf of Aqaba. Flat water, warm air, and spectacular desert scenery. A growing wing foil scene with rental availability. Budget-friendly with excellent food.

7. El Gouna, Egypt

Flat-water lagoon with consistent north wind. Shallow, sandy, and beginner-friendly for wing foiling. Resort infrastructure with good accommodation options.

8. Croatia (Bol, Viganj)

The Maestral thermal blows reliably in summer along the Adriatic coast. Bol on Brac island and Viganj on the Peljesac peninsula offer clean, cross-shore conditions. Crystal-clear water and mild temperatures.

9. Sardinia, Italy

Porto Pollo in the north offers Mistral-driven wind in a sheltered channel between Sardinia and Corsica. Flat water, warm conditions, and a relaxed Italian atmosphere.

10. Netherlands (Workum, Hindeloopen)

The IJsselmeer provides flat, shallow water with North Sea-driven wind. Cold water (12–18°C) but consistent wind and well-developed water sports infrastructure. The Netherlands' flat landscape means clean wind without turbulence.

Gear for Wing Foil Travel

Wing foil gear is relatively compact for travel — one wing packs to carry-on size, and many boards are available in inflatable travel versions. Key tip: use a padded foil bag to protect mast and wings during flights.

FAQ

Q: What's the minimum wind for wing foiling?
A: With a large wing (6.0m+) and efficient foil, experienced riders can wing foil in 10–12 knots. Beginners need 14–18 knots.

Q: Is wing foil gear easy to travel with?
A: Easier than windsurf gear — wings pack small, and many foil masts break down. A dedicated travel bag is recommended for the foil.

Q: Do I need different gear for each spot?
A: One 5.0m wing, a mid-volume board, and a versatile foil covers most European destinations for an 80kg rider.
excerpt: Top 10 wing foil spots in Europe ranked by wind, water conditions, and overall experience.

title: Windsurfing in El Gouna Egypt — Spot Guide
meta_title: Windsurfing El Gouna 2026 — Lagoon & Wind Guide
meta_description: Windsurfing El Gouna Egypt: flat-water lagoon, year-round wind, warm water. The perfect winter escape. Surf Store guide.
meta_keywords: windsurfing el gouna,el gouna windsurf,egypt windsurfing,el gouna lagoon
body_html:
El Gouna — Europe's Winter Windsurf Escape

El Gouna is a purpose-built resort town on Egypt's Red Sea coast, 25km north of Hurghada. For European windsurfers escaping winter, it offers what home spots can't: reliable 15–25 knot north wind, flat lagoon water, 25–30°C air temperature, and water warm enough for a shorty wetsuit year-round.

The windsurf infrastructure is excellent — multiple professional centres offer gear rental, storage, and instruction. The shallow lagoon with sandy bottom makes El Gouna one of the safest spots in the world to progress your windsurfing skills.

The Spots

Mangroovy Beach: The main windsurf station. Flat lagoon water, side-shore wind, and shallow depth extending 500m from shore. Ideal for freeriding, freestyle, and foil windsurfing. Multiple stations with current gear from Duotone and NeilPryde.

Buzzha Beach: Slightly north. Similar conditions with fewer riders. Good for kiters and windsurfers sharing space.

Sliders Bay: Wave spot outside the lagoon. Cross-shore wind with Red Sea swell. For experienced riders comfortable in 1–2m waves.

Wind & Weather

MonthWind (kts)Air TempWater TempRating
Jan–Mar15–2220–25°C20–22°CGood
Apr–Jun18–2828–35°C24–28°CExcellent
Jul–Sep15–2535–40°C28–30°CGood (hot)
Oct–Dec15–2225–30°C24–26°CGood

Gear Advice

For an 80kg rider, bring sails in the 5.5–7.5m range. A 6.5m covers the majority of sessions. Board: 110–130L freeride. Wetsuit: a 2mm shorty or lycra is sufficient October–May; boardshorts work June–September. UV protection is essential — the Egyptian sun is intense.

FAQ

Q: Best month to windsurf El Gouna?
A: April–June for the strongest wind with pleasant temperatures. October–November for warm, uncrowded sessions.

Q: Can I rent gear in El Gouna?
A: Yes — multiple pro stations offer current-model Duotone and NeilPryde gear. Quality is excellent.

Q: Do I need a visa for Egypt?
A: Most EU citizens can get a visa on arrival at Hurghada airport for ~$25.
excerpt: Complete guide to windsurfing in El Gouna Egypt: spots, wind chart, and travel tips.
